mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending power, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE rises above the 0.2pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous monthly growth in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized strength in consumer demand and residential investment, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint positive signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns with a statistical significance level of p < 0.10 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the index of industrial production signals weakening manufacturing output and supply-side contraction, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days during periods of high inflation.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines produce positive 20-day forward returns in high inflation regimes, or the predictive alpha vanishes when CPIAUCSL exceeds 8.0.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate level above 4.0% combined with stable inflation expectations reduces recession probability and supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative in 60% or more of periods where FEDFUNDS remains above 4.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between declining total nonfarm payrolls and rising unemployment indicates labor market deterioration and reduced corporate earnings expectations, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines MoM while UNRATE rises MoM
failure_condition: The divergent labor signal fails to predict negative 60-day returns, with a hit rate below 45% in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real gross domestic product reflects expanding aggregate demand and corporate revenue fundamentals, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show zero or negative correlation with 20-day S&P 500 returns across multiple business cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Declines in the industrial production index signal manufacturing weakness that disproportionately harms equity valuations during periods of tight financial conditions, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines fail to predict negative 5-day returns when credit spreads exceed 150 basis points, or the signal predictive power reverses sign.
